title: Analysis of Golgi Secretory Functions in Cancer.
author_string: Banerjee, Priyam, Tan, Xiaochao, Russell, William K, Kurie, Jonathan M
abstract: Cancer cells utilize secretory pathways for paracrine signaling and extracellular matrix remodeling to facilitate directional cell migration, invasion, and metastasis. The Golgi apparatus is a central secretory signaling hub that is often deregulated in cancer. Here we described technologies that utilize microscopic, biochemical, and proteomic approaches to analyze Golgi secretory functions in genetically heterogeneous cancer cell lines.
